case_file.observation: After a refund, the invoice total drops by the refund amount but the customer's balance ledger entry remains positive.
case_file.expected_invariant: Refund must decrement the customer balance ledger entry by the same amount it decrements the invoice total.
case_file.audited_claim: applyRefund() in billing-service.ts:142 decrements the invoice but does not write a balancing ledger entry.
case_file.falsifier: A reproduction that calls applyRefund, then reads the ledger, and observes a matching negative entry. If that holds, C0 fails.

fusion.root_cause: applyRefund mutates the invoice total but does not enqueue a corresponding ledger entry. The code path is the only path that writes refunds; no compensating write exists elsewhere in the call chain.
fusion.terminal_impact: Customer balance overstates by the refund amount for every refunded invoice until manual reconciliation.

evidence.result: Method updates invoice.total but does not call ledger.recordEntry or enqueue a balancing write.

evidence.method: Run the new test in an unfixed checkout.
evidence.result: Test asserts balance drops by refund amount. Fails: balance unchanged after refund.

evidence.method: Captured production trace replayed in staging.
evidence.result: Span shows invoice write only; no ledger span emitted.
Spec requires every invoice mutation to produce a balancing ledger entry within the same transaction.

links.mechanism: Direct assignment of refund amount to invoice total.
applyRefund returns without invoking the ledger writer; static grep confirms no call.
Spec requires balancing entry per invoice mutation; runtime trace shows no such entry.

confirmed_defects.claim: applyRefund does not write a balancing ledger entry.
confirmed_defects.root_cause: Missing ledger.recordEntry call in the refund write path; no compensating write exists.
confirmed_defects.impact: Customer balance overstates by refund amount for every refund until manual reconciliation; financial reporting drift.

disproven_concerns.evidence_of_absence: Read of recordEntry body shows it raises on write failure; no try/catch swallow.
disproven_concerns.scope_limits: Confirmed for the recordEntry signature used by the refund path; alternative signatures not enumerated.

non_center_observations.observation: applyCredit appears to follow the same write-only pattern; this audit did not extend scope.
non_center_observations.exclusion_reason: Out of scope: observation references applyCredit, which is a separate invariant surface. Flagged for a follow-up audit, not folded into this finding.

indirect: packages/billing/src/reports/monthly-balance.ts [aggregates ledger entries; understates balance until reconciliation]
boundary: public billing API: POST /v1/refunds (caller observes 200 OK; invariant violated internally)

repair_contract.objective: Add a balancing ledger.recordEntry call inside the same transaction as the refund write, so every refunded invoice produces a matching negative balance entry.
repair_contract.required_invariant: After applyRefund completes successfully, the customer balance ledger entry must be decremented by exactly the refund amount within the same transaction.
forbidden_scope: packages/billing/src/ledger.ts [recordEntry]: the writer is correct; do not change its signature or swallow semantics
packages/billing/src/billing-service.ts [applyCredit]: separate invariant surface; flag for follow-up, do not fold in
compatibility: All existing successful-refund calls must continue to return 200 OK
Refunds with no prior balance must continue to produce a negative entry (no special-case skip)
Idempotency: retrying a refund must not produce duplicate ledger entries
repair_contract.reversibility: Single-function edit + one new test file. Revert by git revert of the fix commit. No schema migration, no config flag, no data backfill required (the bug currently leaves overstatements; the fix stops creating new ones).

failing_before.description: refund-balance.test.ts:runRefund fails — balance unchanged after refund

passing_after.description: refund-balance.test.ts:runRefund passes — balance drops by refund amount
Existing refund tests remain green (no regression in successful path)

impacted_checks.description: Ledger reconciliation job detects drift within 1h
impacted_checks.method: Run reconciliation against staging; observe zero drift introduced by the fix path

independent_witness.independence: Distinct evidence channel from code-static (E1) and test-runtime (E2); runtime trace was captured from staging replay, not from the failing test or the static read.
independent_witness.result: Trace span confirms no ledger call is emitted during applyRefund; corroborates E1 and E2.

non_scope: applyCredit (separate invariant surface; flag for follow-up audit)
Reconciliation logic (drift detection works; the bug is upstream of it)
Public billing API contract (no change required)

skipped: applyRefund's retry wrapper (proven not to write)
applyCredit body (separate invariant surface; excluded by repair contract)

stop_reason: Defect trajectory proven CERTAIN at HIGH confidence through three independent evidence channels (code-static, test-runtime, runtime trace) plus the contract spec. Frontier ended at Stop 9 with failing-before test in place. Repair contract handed off.
